topic: Fable-5 efficiency: extract net-new fable-mode and opus-fable-mode mechanisms, map adjustable Public-repo surfaces by read reliability, and rank deduped surface x delta recommendations.

focus: Initialize the charter and dedupe against round-1 shipped doctrine.
noveltyJustification: First pass established the baseline doctrine, the round-1 shipped set, and the net-new gap taxonomy.

ruledOut.approach: Re-recommend the round-1 AGENTS/constitutional/sk-code distribution as the main deliverable
ruledOut.reason: Round 1 already shipped those surfaces; round 2 must focus on net-new mechanisms and measurement.

focus: Extract net-new techniques from fable-mode-main profile and command overlay.
noveltyJustification: The profile adds rituals and failure modes not present in the distilled Fable5.md note: mutation checks, adversarial review, scar-tissue handoffs, and two-register operation.
keyQuestions: Which fable-mode-main techniques are net-new versus Fable5.md?
Which techniques are doctrine, ritual, mechanism, or measurement?
answeredQuestions: Which fable-mode-main techniques are net-new versus Fable5.md?

ruledOut.approach: Install a slash-command-only fable mode as the primary Public mechanism
ruledOut.reason: The source command is on-demand and session-local; Public needs persistent cross-runtime influence for default behavior.

focus: Extract opus-fable-mode governor, re-injection, and measurement mechanisms.
noveltyJustification: This pass converted the Opus source into concrete mechanism and metric candidates: setpoint, thermostat, and leak-test measurement.
keyQuestions: Which metrics can verify behavioral movement?
answeredQuestions: Which metrics can verify behavioral movement?

ruledOut.approach: Treat prompt text as a capability upgrade
ruledOut.reason: The source explicitly says prompts steer working style but do not change the model's capability ceiling.

focus: Map adjustable Public surfaces by read reliability and runtime reach.
noveltyJustification: Surface inventory found the strongest persistent mechanism: prompt-time hooks and advisor bridge, plus lower-reliability skill, agent, and command surfaces.
keyQuestions: Which surfaces have high read reliability per runtime?
answeredQuestions: Which surfaces have high read reliability per runtime?

ruledOut.approach: Scatter new doctrine across every agent and command first
ruledOut.reason: Agent and command files are narrower and more drift-prone than always-read root instructions and prompt-time hook injection.

focus: Rank surface x delta recommendations by leverage, cost, blast radius, and dedup status.
noveltyJustification: Most evidence was confirmatory, but the ranking insight is new: mechanism and measurement outrank more doctrine text for round 2.

ruledOut.approach: Put the behavioral governor only in AGENTS.md
ruledOut.reason: AGENTS.md is already dense and read once; the Opus source says a memory line decays and needs per-prompt re-injection.

focus: Validate convergence, document runtime caveats, and finalize synthesis.
noveltyJustification: Only residual novelty remained: executor-provenance caveats and final eliminated alternatives; all key questions had evidence-backed answers.
keyQuestions: What runtime caveats must be flagged?
Can this lineage converge before maxIterations?
answeredQuestions: What runtime caveats must be flagged?
Can this lineage converge before maxIterations?

ruledOut.approach: Claim this lineage actually spawned nested cli-codex gpt-5.5 processes
ruledOut.reason: The cli-codex skill forbids self-invocation from inside Codex; the requested executor is recorded, but actual execution is direct-current Codex.

ruledOut.approach: Run spec write-back or memory save from this leaf
ruledOut.reason: The fan-out instruction freezes writes to the lineage artifact_dir only; spec write-back and memory save would touch parent spec docs.
